Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx

Class FlixelGame

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelGame
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.ApplicationListener, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelDrawable, FlixelUpdatable
public abstract class FlixelGame extends Object implements com.badlogic.gdx.ApplicationListener, FlixelUpdatable, FlixelDrawable, FlixelDestroyable
The game object used for containing the main loop and core elements of the Flixel game.

To actually use this properly, you need to create a subclass of this and override the methods you want to change.

It is strongly advised that you do NOT use this class to add the main gameplay logic to your game; your code should go into your FlixelState or libGDX Screen classes instead.

It is recommended for using this in the following way: 


 // Create a new subclass of FlixelGame.
 // Remember that you can override any methods to add extra functionality
 // to the game's behavior.
 public class MyGame extends FlixelGame {

   public MyGame(String title, int width, int height, FlixelState initialState) {
     super(title, width, height, initialState);
   }
 }
Then, in a platform-specific launcher, you can create a new instance of your game and run it: 

 // Example of how to create a new game instance and run it using the LWJGL3 launcher.
 public class Lwjgl3Launcher {

   public static void main(String[] args) {
     if (StartupHelper.startNewJvmIfRequired()) { // This handles macOS support and helps on Windows.
       return;
     }

     MyGame game = new MyGame(
       "My Game",
       800,
       600,
       new InitialState() // The initial state the game enters when it starts!
     );

     FlixelLwjgl3Launcher.launch(game);
   }
 }

    Description
    boolean
    autoPause
    Should the game pause update calls and audio when the window loses focus or is minimized?

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    FlixelGame(String title, int width, int height, @NotNull Supplier<FlixelState> initialStateFactory, int framerate, boolean vsync, boolean fullscreen)
    Same as FlixelGame(String, int, int, FlixelState, int, boolean, boolean) but supplies a new root state from a factory (recommended after Flixel.resetGame() so each cold start can use new MyState()).

    Creates a new game instance with the details specified.

  • Field Details

    • autoPause

      public boolean autoPause
      Should the game pause update calls and audio when the window loses focus or is minimized?

  • Constructor Details

    • FlixelGame

      public FlixelGame(String title, FlixelState initialScreen)
      Creates a new game instance with the details specified.
      Parameters:
      title - The title of the game's window.
      initialScreen - The initial screen to load when the game starts.

    • FlixelGame

      public FlixelGame(String title, int width, int height, FlixelState initialScreen)
      Creates a new game instance with the details specified.
      Parameters:
      title - The title of the game's window.
      width - The starting width of the game's window and how wide the camera should be.
      height - The starting height of the game's window and how tall the camera should be.
      initialScreen - The initial screen to load when the game starts.

    • FlixelGame

      public FlixelGame(String title, int width, int height, FlixelState initialScreen, int framerate)
      Creates a new game instance with the details specified.
      Parameters:
      title - The title of the game's window.
      width - The starting width of the game's window and how wide the camera should be.
      height - The starting height of the game's window and how tall the camera should be.
      initialScreen - The initial screen to load when the game starts.
      framerate - The framerate of how fast the game should update and render.

    • FlixelGame

      public FlixelGame(String title, int width, int height, FlixelState initialScreen, int framerate, boolean vsync)
      Creates a new game instance with the details specified.
      Parameters:
      title - The title of the game's window.
      width - The starting width of the game's window and how wide the camera should be.
      height - The starting height of the game's window and how tall the camera should be.
      initialScreen - The initial screen to load when the game starts.
      framerate - The framerate of how fast the game should update and render.
      vsync - Should the game use VSync to limit the framerate to the monitor's refresh rate?

    • FlixelGame

      public FlixelGame(String title, int width, int height, FlixelState initialScreen, int framerate, boolean vsync, boolean fullscreen)
      Creates a new game instance with the details specified.
      Parameters:
      title - The title of the game's window.
      width - The starting width of the game's window and how wide the camera should be.
      height - The starting height of the game's window and how tall the camera should be.
      initialScreen - The initial screen to load when the game starts.
      framerate - The framerate of how fast the game should update and render.
      vsync - Should the game use VSync to limit the framerate to the monitor's refresh rate?
      fullscreen - Should the game start in fullscreen mode?

    • FlixelGame

      public FlixelGame(String title, int width, int height, @NotNull @NotNull Supplier<FlixelState> initialStateFactory, int framerate, boolean vsync, boolean fullscreen)
      Same as FlixelGame(String, int, int, FlixelState, int, boolean, boolean) but supplies a new root state from a factory (recommended after Flixel.resetGame() so each cold start can use new MyState()).
      Parameters:
      initialStateFactory - Non-null supplier; invoked from create() to obtain the root state.

  • Method Details

    • create

      public void create()
      Called when the game is created. This is where you should initialize your game's resources.

      This method configures the crash handler, sets up input processing, initializes the debug overlay, configures the ANSI system for color output in terminals, and then switches to the initial screen.

      This method is called automatically by libGDX's ApplicationListener.create() method when the game is created, so it is not necessary to call this method manually in most cases. However, it can be overridden to perform custom initialization before the game is created.

      Specified by:
      create in interface com.badlogic.gdx.ApplicationListener
      See Also:
      • ApplicationListener.create()

    • resize

      public void resize(int width, int height)
      Specified by:
      resize in interface com.badlogic.gdx.ApplicationListener

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Updates the logic of the game's loop.
      Specified by:
      update in interface FlixelUpdatable
      Parameters:
      elapsed - The amount of time that occurred in the last frame.

    • draw

      public void draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch)
      Updates the graphics and display of the game.
      Specified by:
      draw in interface FlixelDrawable
      Parameters:
      batch - The batch to use for drawing the game.

    • render

      public void render()
      Updates the game's global and internal update(float) and draw(Batch) methods, with elapsed time clamped to the min and max values to prevent major lag spikes.

      This method is called automatically by libGDX's ApplicationListener.render() method when the game is running, so it is not necessary to override this method in most cases. However, it can be overridden to perform custom updating/rendering before the game is updated/rendered.

      This method is kept non-final for compatibility with existing projects that want to extend to this class and use its structured game loop. It's advised to override update(float) and draw(Batch) instead to perform custom updating and rendering.

      Specified by:
      render in interface com.badlogic.gdx.ApplicationListener
      See Also:
      • update(float)
      • draw(Batch)
      • ApplicationListener.render()

    • isGamePaused

      public boolean isGamePaused()

    • setGamePaused

      public void setGamePaused(boolean gamePaused)
      Pauses the game's update loop. This is mostly used by the debugger, although you might find it useful for other purposes.
      Parameters:
      gamePaused - Whether the game should be paused or not.

    • pause

      public void pause()
      Specified by:
      pause in interface com.badlogic.gdx.ApplicationListener

    • resume

      public void resume()
      Specified by:
      resume in interface com.badlogic.gdx.ApplicationListener

    • onWindowFocused

      public void onWindowFocused()
      Called when the user regains focus on the game's window.

    • onWindowUnfocused

      public void onWindowUnfocused()
      Called when the user loses focus on the game's window, while also not being minimized.

    • onWindowMinimized

      public void onWindowMinimized(boolean iconified)
      Called when the user minimizes the game's window.
      Parameters:
      iconified - Whether the window is iconified (minimized) or not. This parameter is provided for compatibility with the window listener in the LWJGL3 (desktop) launcher.

    • setFullscreen

      public void setFullscreen(boolean enabled)
      Sets fullscreen mode for the game's window.
      Parameters:
      enabled - If the game's window should be in fullscreen mode.

    • toggleFullscreen

      public void toggleFullscreen()
      Toggles fullscreen mode on or off, depending on the current state.

    • toggleAutoPause

      public boolean toggleAutoPause()
      Toggles auto-pause on or off.
      Returns:
      The new value of autoPause after toggling.

    • dispose

      public void dispose()
      Specified by:
      dispose in interface com.badlogic.gdx.ApplicationListener
      See Also:
      • destroy()

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Destroys the game and all of its resources. Note that this closes the game entirely. If you want to reset the game without closing it, use reset() instead.
      Specified by:
      destroy in interface FlixelDestroyable
      See Also:
      • reset()

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Tears down this session's cameras, state, stage, and batch without application-exit semantics. No Flixel.stopFileLogging(), ANSI uninstall, or postGameClose signal, and does not dispose Flixel.assets or Flixel.sound (callers such as Flixel.resetGame() own those). Leaves isClosed() false so the process can call Flixel.initialize(FlixelGame) again.

    • setLogsFolder

      public void setLogsFolder(String absolutePathToLogsFolder)
      Sets a custom folder for log files. Call before create() so that file logging uses this folder instead of the default (project root in IDE, directory containing the JAR when run from a JAR).
      Parameters:
      absolutePathToLogsFolder - Absolute path to the logs folder, or null to use the default.

    • getCamera

      public FlixelCamera getCamera()
      Gets the first camera that is part of the list. If the list is null or empty, then a new list (with a default camera accordingly) is created.
      Returns:
      The first camera in the list.

    • resetCameras

      public void resetCameras()
      Resets the camera list to contain a single default camera with the current window size as its viewport.
